sections.label: Section 769
sections.original: τί δʼ ὧδʼ ἀθυμεῖς , ὦ γύναι , τῷ νῦν λόγῳ ;
sections.translation: Why are you so despondent , lady , at my news ?

sections.label: Section 770
sections.original: δεινὸν τὸ τίκτειν ἐστίν · οὐδὲ γὰρ κακῶς
sections.translation: There is a terrible power in motherhood ; a mother may be wronged , but she can feel no hate for those whom she bore .

sections.label: Section 772
sections.original: μάτην ἄρʼ ἡμεῖς , ὡς ἔοικεν , ἥκομεν .
sections.translation: Then it seems that we have come in vain .

sections.label: Section 773
sections.original: οὔτοι μάτην γε · πῶς γὰρ ἂν μάτην λέγοις ,
sections.translation: No , not in vain ; how can you say in vain when you have brought me sure proofs of his death ?

sections.label: Section 775
sections.original: προσῆλθες , ὅστις τῆς ἐμῆς ψυχῆς γεγώς ,
sections.translation: He sprang from my own life , yet deserting my breast and my nurture he became a fugitive , completely alien from me . And me , once he left this land , he saw no more ; but , charging me with the murder of his father , he made terrible threats ,

sections.label: Section 780
sections.original: ὥστʼ οὔτε νυκτὸς ὕπνον οὔτʼ ἐξ ἡμέρας
sections.translation: so that neither by night nor by day could sweet sleep cover me , but the imminent moment made me live always as if I were about to die . Now , however , since today I am rid of terror of him and of this girl — that greater plague

sections.label: Section 785
sections.original: ξύνοικος ἦν μοι , τοὐμὸν ἐκπίνουσʼ ἀεὶ
sections.translation: who shared my home while consuming undiluted my life-blood — now , I think , for all her threats , I shall pass my days in peace .

sections.label: Section 788
sections.original: οἴμοι τάλαινα · νῦν γὰρ οἰμῶξαι πάρα ,
sections.translation: Ah , what misery ! Now , indeed , Orestes , I must mourn your misfortune , since even dead as you are

sections.label: Section 790
sections.original: πρὸς τῆσδʼ ὑβρίζει μητρός . ἆρʼ ἔχει καλῶς ;
sections.translation: you are abused by this woman , your mother ! Is it not just fine ?

sections.label: Section 791
sections.original: οὔτοι σύ · κεῖνος δʼ ὡς ἔχει καλῶς ἔχει .
sections.translation: You certainly are not , but he is fine as he is .

sections.label: Section 792
sections.translation: Listen to her , Nemesis of the recently departed !

sections.label: Section 793
sections.translation: She has heard who should be heard , and has ordained well .

sections.label: Section 794
sections.translation: Abuse us ! Fortune is with you today .

sections.label: Section 795
sections.original: οὔκουν Ὀρέστης καὶ σὺ παύσετον τάδε .
sections.translation: You and Orestes will not stop me , then , will you ?

sections.label: Section 796
sections.original: πεπαύμεθʼ ἡμεῖς , οὐχ ὅπως σὲ παύσομεν .
sections.translation: It is we who are stopped ; we cannot stop you .

sections.label: Section 797
sections.original: πολλῶν ἂν ἥκοις , ὦ ξένʼ , ἄξιος τυχεῖν ,
sections.translation: Your coming , sir , deserves large recompense , if you have stopped her clamorous tongue .

sections.label: Section 799
sections.original: οὐκοῦν ἀποστείχοιμʼ ἄν , εἰ τάδʼ εὖ κυρεῖ .
sections.translation: Then I would take my leave , if all is well .

sections.label: Section 800
sections.original: ἥκιστʼ · ἐπείπερ οὔτʼ ἐμοῦ κατάξιʼ ἂν
sections.translation: Not so ; your welcome would then be unworthy of me , and of my ally who sent you . No , come in . Leave her out here to shout out loud her misfortunes and those of her friends . Clytaemnestra and the Paedagogus enter the house .

sections.label: Section 804
sections.translation: What do you think ? Does it seem to you that she , poor woman , wept and wailed terribly ,

sections.label: Section 805
sections.translation: like a grieving , anguished woman , over her son thus destroyed ? No , she left us with a laugh ! Ah , miserable me ! Dearest Orestes , how your death has destroyed me ! For your passing has torn from my heart

sections.label: Section 810
sections.original: αἵ μοι μόναι παρῆσαν ἐλπίδων ἔτι ,
sections.translation: the only hopes which still were mine : that you would live to return some day as the avenger of our father , and also of me in my misery . But now , where shall I turn ? I am alone , cheated of you , as of my father . Hereafter I must be a slave again

sections.label: Section 815
sections.translation: among those I most hate , my father ’ s murderers . Am I not in a fine way ? But at least in the time remaining me I will never enter the house to dwell with them . No , lying down at these gates , without a friend , I shall wither away my days .

sections.label: Section 820
sections.original: πρὸς ταῦτα καινέτω τις , εἰ βαρύνεται ,
sections.translation: Therefore , if anyone in the house be angry , let him kill me . It is a favor , if I die , but a pain , if I live . I desire life no more .

sections.label: Section 1.823
sections.original: ποῦ ποτε κεραυνοὶ Διὸς ἢ ποῦ φαέθων
sections.translation: Where are the thunderbolts of Zeus , or where the

sections.label: Section 1.825
sections.original: Ἅλιος , εἰ ταῦτʼ ἐφορῶντες κρύπτουσιν ἕκηλοι ;
sections.translation: shining Sun , if they look upon these things and quietly cover them over ?

sections.label: Section 1.827
sections.translation: Ah , me , ah , me !

sections.label: Section 1.828
sections.translation: My child , why do you weep ?

sections.label: Section 1.830
sections.translation: Give no cry of bad omen !

sections.label: Section 1.831
sections.translation: How do you mean ?

sections.label: Section 1.832
sections.translation: If you suggest that I keep hope for those who have surely passed to Hades ,

sections.label: Section 1.835
sections.translation: you will trample even harder upon me as I waste away .

sections.label: Section 1.836
sections.translation: No , for I know that the prince Amphiaraus was ensnared by a woman ’ s chain of gold and swallowed up . And now beneath the earth —

sections.label: Section 1.840
sections.translation: ah , me , ah , me !

sections.label: Section 1.841
sections.translation: — He reigns supreme with the wits of the living .

sections.label: Section 1.842
sections.translation: ah , me !

sections.label: Section 1.843
sections.translation: ah , me , indeed ! For the murderess —

sections.label: Section 1.844
sections.translation: Was slain .

sections.label: Section 1.845
sections.translation: Yes .

sections.label: Section 1.846
sections.translation: I know it ; I know it . For a champion arose to avenge the grieving dead . But for me no champion remains : he who yet remained has been snatched clean away .

sections.label: Section 2.849
sections.translation: Unhappy are you , unhappy your destiny !

sections.label: Section 2.850
sections.translation: How well I know that , all too well , with my life swept through all the months by abundant terrors and horrors !

sections.label: Section 2.853
sections.translation: We have witnessed the events for which you mourn .

sections.label: Section 2.855
sections.translation: Cease , then , to divert me from it , since no longer —

sections.label: Section 2.857
sections.translation: — Since I no longer have hope in my brother , the seed of our shared noble line , to aid me .

sections.label: Section 2.860
sections.translation: Nature ordains death as the destiny of all mortals .

sections.label: Section 2.861
sections.translation: What , a death like the one which that ill-fated one died beneath a race of swift hooves , entangled in the cutting , dragging reins ?

sections.label: Section 2.864
sections.translation: The mutilation is beyond thought !

sections.label: Section 2.865
sections.translation: Yes , so it is , when in foreign soil , without being tended by my hands —

sections.label: Section 2.867
sections.translation: Ah , no !

sections.label: Section 2.868
sections.translation: — he has been buried not receiving from me either burial

sections.label: Section 2.870
sections.translation: or lamentation .

sections.label: Section 871
sections.translation: I am pursued by joy , dear sister , and I disregard seemliness in order to come with speed . I bring joyful news to relieve your former troubles and grief .

sections.label: Section 875
sections.original: πόθεν δʼ ἂν εὕροις τῶν ἐμῶν σὺ πημάτων
sections.translation: And from where could you find help for my sufferings , when no cure for them can be imagined ?

sections.label: Section 877
sections.original: πάρεστʼ Ὀρέστης ἡμίν , ἴσθι τοῦτʼ ἐμοῦ
sections.translation: Orestes is with us — know this from my lips — plainly visible , just as you see me now .

sections.label: Section 879
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἦ μέμηνας , ὦ τάλαινα , κἀπὶ τοῖς
sections.translation: What , are you insane , poor girl ?

sections.label: Section 880
sections.original: σαυτῆς κακοῖσι κἀπὶ τοῖς ἐμοῖς γελᾷς ;
sections.translation: Do you laugh at my sorrows and your own ?

sections.label: Section 881
sections.original: μὰ τὴν πατρῴαν ἑστίαν , ἀλλʼ οὐχ ὕβρει
sections.translation: No , by our father ’ s hearth , I do not speak in mockery . I tell you that he truly is with us .

sections.label: Section 883
sections.original: οἴμοι τάλαινα · καὶ τίνος βροτῶν λόγον
sections.translation: Ah , miserable girl ! And from whom on earth have you heard this tale , which you believe so lightly ?

sections.label: Section 885
sections.original: ἐγὼ μὲν ἐξ ἐμοῦ τε κοὐκ ἄλλης , σαφῆ
sections.translation: I have it on my own knowledge , no one else ’ s ; I have seen clear proofs .

sections.label: Section 887
sections.original: τίνʼ , ὦ τάλαινʼ , ἔχουσα πίστιν ; ἐς τί μοι
sections.translation: What have you seen , poor girl , to warrant your belief ? What did you see , that you are warmed by this unquenchable fire ?
